title: senna caudata var. diadena
intro: Senna caudata var. diadena, also known as the 'wild senna', is a plant species in the Fabaceae family that is native to tropical Africa and Southeast Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its laxative and purgative effects, which are attributed to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, specifically diadenin. The health benefits of Senna caudata var. diadena include relief from constipation, as well as its potential use as a treatment for jaundice, fever, and other gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and pods are often used to make teas, infusions, or extracts that can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
